summary.intent: Investigate and fix a CI failure in the moltis repo, distinguishing between a consistent test failure and a flaky one.
summary.outcome: Fixed two E2E test issues: removed an unnecessary `sessions.clear_all` RPC call that caused timeouts under CI load in sessions.spec.js, and bumped a marginal 5000ms timeout in channels-nostr.spec.js to reduce flakiness.

repo: sessions.clear_all iterates all accumulated sessions from prior tests and runs heavy per-session cleanup (file I/O, SQLite, sandbox, hooks), making it expensive under CI load
The archive toggle test uses a unique data-session-key selector, so it doesn't require clearing other sessions before running
The project uses biome for JS formatting
E2E tests are Playwright-based, configured in crates/web/ui/playwright.config.js with a 45s default test timeout
The gateway uses an RPC dispatch registry pattern; sessions.clear_all is a registered RPC method

code.finding: Archived sessions toggle test was calling sessions.clear_all RPC unnecessarily at the start, causing timeout under CI load
A 5000ms timeout was marginal for CI, causing intermittent flakiness — bumped to reduce failures

workflow: Distinguish consistent failures from flaky ones early when triaging CI — they require different investigation strategies
Run biome format on changed JS files before committing
Check git log for recent commits touching relevant files to understand context before diving into code

friction: Tracing the RPC dispatch path required reading many files (dispatch.rs, services.rs, ws.rs) with multiple grep iterations before the full call chain was clear
Multiple repeated Grep calls for the same terms (e.g., clear_all, UNAVAILABLE) suggest the initial searches returned incomplete or ambiguous results
open_items: The underlying performance issue with sessions.clear_all (heavy per-session cleanup on bulk delete) was not fixed — only the test was changed to avoid triggering it
The channels-nostr flaky test timeout was bumped as a workaround; root cause of why the operation is slow under CI was not investigated
